operator_company: Simon Fraser University / Digital Research Alliance of Canada
opportunity_summary: Canada's fastest academic supercomputer. CA$80M investment replacing Cedar. 165,888 cores, 640 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 50PB storage, 19.96 PF peak. Transitioned from air cooling (Cedar) to direct-to-chip liquid cooling. PUE 1.07 inherited from Cedar facility design. TOP500 #78. Powered by clean BC hydro energy.
notes: Located at Cedar Supercomputing Centre, SFU Burnaby campus. Supports 17,000+ users. PUE 1.07 was Cedar's measured efficiency (BC Hydro verified); Fir's liquid cooling should be equal or better. GPU: 640x NVIDIA H100. AMD Epyc 9454 CPUs. Operational as of September 2025. Part of Digital Research Alliance of Canada national infrastructure.

operator_company: University of Toronto / SciNet / Digital Research Alliance of Canada
opportunity_summary: $52M replacement for Niagara supercomputer. 241,056 cores (AMD Turin), 252 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 29PB VAST NVMe storage. 1,700 kW power consumption. Water-cooled with free-air cooling integration. Infrastructure replacement included new plumbing for cooling systems. Launched August 2025.
notes: 1,224 CPU nodes (196 AMD Turin cores each) + 63 GPU nodes (4x H100 80GB each). 768GB RAM/node. NDR InfiniBand 400/800 Gb/s. 34 PF theoretical peak. Replaces Niagara and Mist clusters. MW figure from official SciNet specs (1700 kW). Previous Niagara used water cooling with free-air economizer.

operator_company: Calcul Quebec / ETS / Digital Research Alliance of Canada
opportunity_summary: New supercomputer replacing Narval at ETS Montreal. 136,896 cores, 324 GPUs. TOP500 #192 (GPU), #242 (CPU). Green500 #73. Launched June 2025. Cooling type not publicly disclosed -- Green500 ranking suggests reasonable efficiency. Potential liquid cooling upgrade candidate.
notes: Hosted at Ecole de technologie superieure (ETS), Montreal. Managed by Calcul Quebec. Collaborative deployment across 9 Quebec universities including McGill, UdeM, Laval, Concordia, UQAM, Polytechnique, HEC. Replaces Narval cluster. Cooling specifications not found in public sources -- flagged as data gap.

opportunity_summary: 300MW+ campus scaling to 1GW in Regina, SK. Closed-loop immersion cooling by CoolIT Systems. PUE target 1.05-1.15. WUE near-zero. 7,000+ free cooling hours/year. Islanded micro-grid with gas turbines + renewables + 100MWh battery. Phase 1 (2026-27): 10MW IT, $300M CapEx. 100% Canadian-owned. Bird Construction, TransGas, SaskTel, FNPA partners.
notes: Address: 3300A Portnall Ave, Regina, SK S4S 1A7. Treaty 4 Territory. Saskatchewan corp incorporated March 12, 2025. CEO Erwin Heuck, CTO Garnet Brown. Phase 2 (2028-30): 50-100MW. Phase 3 (2030-34): full 300MW. Phase 4: 1GW hyperscale vision. Power cost CAD 2.8c/kWh marginal generation. Federal ITC + provincial rebates reduce CapEx 10-17%. Tenant-supplied GPU colocation model. MW figure is planned total campus, not yet built.

opportunity_summary: 500kW across 3 data halls, 20+ customers. Acquired from Carbon60 in March 2026 for $1.6M + $150K deferred. Liquid cooling configs supported up to 150kW/rack. Protected B security clearance ready. SSAE16 SOC2 Type2 compliant. First DC in Atlantic Canada for Carrier Connect (5th overall). Small facility in underserved market.
notes: Acquired through subsidiary PureColo Inc. via asset purchase agreement dated Feb 27, 2026, announced March 2, 2026. Carrier Connect expands coast-to-coast from BC to Maritime provinces. Free-air, hot-aisle containment, and liquid cooling configs supported. PCI DSS compliant. MW capacity is 0.5MW (500kW) total across 3 halls.

opportunity_summary: Proposed 380MW AI data center on 120-hectare Spruce Lake Industrial Park site. $2B total investment. 190MW on-site gas-fired plant (VoltaGrid) + 190MW from NB Power grid (from 2028). Pending EIA approval. Significant community opposition over water use and emissions. Target operational early 2028.
notes: NOTE: This may overlap with existing 'Beacon AI Foothills Hub' entry -- verify if same project or different. VoltaGrid is Texas-based. Cooling type not publicly disclosed. At 380MW this is hyperscale and may exceed target profile (<= 10MW) but tracked for market intelligence. Frank McKenna promoting Saint John as DC hub. Dark fibre availability cited as key advantage.

opportunity_summary: 100% Water Cooling System. 12,000 server capacity, 132 full racks. 700 tons AC capacity. 3.5MW diesel generator. Located at 5945 Couture Blvd, Saint-Leonard QC. Opened 2006, acquired by Leaseweb from iWeb in August 2021. Water-cooled facility in Montreal -- potential for cooling technology upgrade.
notes: Address: 5945 Couture Boulevard, Saint-Leonard, QC H1P 1A8. N+1 cooling redundancy. 2500KVA UPS units. Leaseweb also operates MTL-01 (20 Place du Commerce, Nun's Island) and MTL-02 in Montreal. MW figure is from diesel generator capacity, not necessarily total IT load.

opportunity_summary: 1,500 servers hosting public and private sector workloads. 195 Colonnade Road, Ottawa. Canadian sovereign cloud provider. Working to move off Ontario grid to natural gas cogeneration. Equivalent power of 10,000 residential homes. State-of-the-art cooling with redundant units. Global footprint across Canada, US, Bahamas, Australia, UK.
notes: Address: 195 Colonnade Road, Ottawa ON. PUE and MW not publicly disclosed. Moving to natural gas cogeneration could signal infrastructure upgrade. Canadian sovereign cloud focus makes it relevant for government AI workloads. Cooling details are generic -- no liquid cooling evidence found.

opportunity_summary: Tier 3 design carrier-neutral data center in Winkler, MB (115km SW of Winnipeg). Commscope NG4 cross-connect system. Proximity to US border with diverse redundant crossing points. Manitoba's low-cost hydro power. Small facility in underserved rural Manitoba market.
notes: Address: 965 HWY 14, Winkler, MB. Only DC in Winkler. Tier 3 architecture (99.982% availability). No PUE, MW, or cooling details publicly available. Valley Fiber is local ISP/telecom. Potential greenfield liquid cooling opportunity given Manitoba's cold climate (extensive free cooling potential).

opportunity_summary: 7.5MW purpose-built facility. 64,000 sq ft. Uptime Institute Tier III design-certified. Built 2015 for Manitoba Telecom/Bell MTS, acquired by Equinix in $780M deal (13 Bell DCs) in 2020. Equinix rolling out direct-to-chip liquid cooling across 100+ DCs globally -- Winnipeg expansion planned.
notes: Address: 1450 Waverley Street, Winnipeg MB. Equinix announced liquid cooling expansion to Toronto, Calgary, Kamloops -- Winnipeg likely next in line. Equinix global liquid cooling now in 45+ metros. Supports AI and high-density workloads. Manitoba's cold climate ideal for free cooling integration.

opportunity_summary: 2 colocation sites in Halifax, NS. Carrier neutral. Half/quarter/full rack leasing. Up to 100G+ connectivity. 20+ years operating history. Small operator in underserved Atlantic Canada market. Halifax ranked #2 Emerging Tech Market in North America (CBRE).
notes: Address: 2686 Agricola St, Halifax NS. Small operator -- no PUE, MW, or cooling specs publicly disclosed. Nova Scotia positioning itself for DC investment with renewable energy, low costs, and geopolitical stability. Potential greenfield liquid cooling market.

opportunity_summary: TIA-942 Tier III. N+1 redundant cooling. Dual-path environmental control (temp and humidity). Diesel backup generation. Located at 5562 Sackville Street, Halifax. Operated by Internetworking Atlantic Inc (IAI), now Rogers Carrier Services. Enterprise-grade facility in Atlantic Canada.
notes: Address: 5562 Sackville Street, Halifax NS B3J 1L1. No PUE or MW publicly disclosed. Air cooling with CRAC units. N+1 redundancy tied to backup power. Potential liquid cooling retrofit candidate as Rogers expands AI capabilities.

opportunity_summary: Scalable up to 24MW. 2N power, N+1 cooling. High-density rack ready up to 50kW/cabinet. Hot/cold aisle containment with free cold air cooling. Montreal location leverages Quebec climate for PUE efficiency. Canadian dedicated server and colocation provider.
notes: 24MW is scalable capacity, not necessarily current draw. Free air cooling is primary method. No liquid cooling evidence found. 50kW/rack density suggests high-density readiness. MW figure marked unverified as it represents maximum scalable capacity.

opportunity_summary: $250M Sovereign AI Research Hub at Hypertec's LaSalle HQ. Direct-to-chip + immersion cooling + applied heat recovery reducing energy consumption by up to 50%. TRIDENT iC Series single-phase immersion servers. Immersion-as-a-Service (IaaS) platform with Intel Xeon 5th Gen. Partnership with Mila (Quebec AI Institute) and 5C Group.
notes: DEDUP: Overlaps with existing '5C/Hypertec/Mila LaSalle Campus' -- this entry ADDS immersion cooling details (TRIDENT iC Series, Immersion-aaS, heat recovery) not in original record. Merge into existing record. Hypertec sold hyperscale DC operations to Vantage Data Centers previously. Hypertec manufactures TRIDENT immersion servers. Hypertec Cloud launched Immersion-aaS in April 2024. Also partner on Nibi supercomputer at UWaterloo.

opportunity_summary: 4 data centers in downtown Winnipeg on private fibre network. Manages Manitoba Internet Exchange (MBix). Carrier neutral colocation. 100M/1G/10G connections. Largest DC operator in Manitoba by site count. Small server rooms in Exchange District.
notes: Address: 130 Portage Ave E, Winnipeg MB (YWG1). Also operates YWG2 (294 Portage Ave), YWG4 (355 Portage Ave). Small-scale operator. Raised floors, redundant AC, backup UPS and generator. No liquid cooling evidence. Manitoba's cold climate = strong free cooling potential.

opportunity_summary: 2MW carrier-neutral data center at 390 March Road, Kanata ON. Supports free-air, hot-aisle containment, and liquid cooling configs up to 150kW/rack. Protected B security clearance ready. Part of Carrier Connect's 5-DC national portfolio. Ottawa government/tech hub location.
notes: Address: 390 March Road, Ottawa/Kanata ON. Liquid cooling infrastructure supports up to 150kW/rack configurations but unclear if currently deployed vs. available. SSAE16 SOC2 Type2 and CSAE3416 Type2 compliant. PCI DSS compliant. Ottawa is major Canadian government IT hub.

opportunity_summary: 300MW AI data center campus in Ontario. Largest two-phase direct-to-chip deployment to date. Accelsius NeuCool technology: 4500W+ per socket, 0.020 C/W thermal resistance at 700W+ TDP (H100 GPU). Waterless, low-pressure dielectric refrigerant (zero ODP). Johnson Controls chillers for facility cooling. Phase 1: two 65MW facilities in 2026-2027.
notes: DEDUP: Overlaps with existing 'DarkNX Ontario Campus' -- this entry ADDS Accelsius NeuCool cooling partnership details (two-phase DLC, 4500W/socket, JCI chillers) not in original record. Merge into existing record. Announced November 2025. Exact Ontario location not disclosed. DarkNX is new digital infrastructure company. NeuCool uses non-conductive refrigerants for chip-level cooling. Accelsius is an Innventure portfolio company.

opportunity_summary: Vancouver-based cleantech startup. Patent-pending closed-loop liquid-cooling using phase change materials (PCM) and AI-driven energy optimization. 80%+ cooling energy savings. Zero water waste. SuperPODs with proprietary passive PCM cooling compatible with immersion, DLC, or air-to-liquid. No chillers or refrigerants needed. Strategy: retrofit existing halls then build net-zero GPU farms.
notes: Developing prototype facility in Canada (exact location TBD). 4 provisional patents in advanced thermal storage and hybrid trigeneration. Third-party lab validation underway. Very early stage -- no operational facility yet. Relevant as potential cooling technology partner/competitor. Vancouver HQ but facility location may differ.

opportunity_summary: Cerebras announced data center in Manitoba (details scarce). Cerebras uses proprietary wafer-scale chips in custom hardware. CEO Andrew Feldman announced at GTC reception. Manitoba's cheap hydro ($0.07/kWh industrial) and cold climate attractive. Separate from Cerebras' tenant role at Bell Saskatchewan 300MW campus.
notes: Very limited public information. Location, MW, cooling type all undisclosed. Cerebras also named as tenant at Bell's 300MW Saskatchewan facility. Wafer-scale chips have unique thermal profiles. Manitoba also seeing Bell/HIVE BUZZ 5MW GPU cluster. Track for future details.

facility_name: Bell AI Fabric Saskatchewan (RM of Sherwood)
opportunity_summary: 300MW campus on 160 acres in RM of Sherwood outside Regina. $1.7B investment. $10B compute hardware from tenants. CoreWeave (NVIDIA GPUs) and Cerebras (wafer-scale) as anchor tenants. Closed-loop cooling -- zero municipal water draw. Heat recovery planned with University of Regina greenhouses and George Gordon First Nation. Multiple 50MW buildings totaling ~501,345 sq ft. First stage online H1 2027. Bell's largest-ever Saskatchewan investment.
notes: DEDUP: Overlaps with existing 'Bell AI Fabric Regina' -- this entry ADDS detailed information: $1.7B investment, $10B compute from tenants (CoreWeave NVIDIA GPUs + Cerebras wafer-scale), 160 acres, multiple 50MW buildings, 501,345 sq ft, heat recovery with UofR greenhouses and George Gordon First Nation, H1 2027 online, closed-loop zero municipal water. Merge into existing record. Land rezoned AG to light industrial. Construction start 2026.

opportunity_summary: 200,000 sq ft data center proposed in Nanaimo, BC (Vancouver Island). Operator runs similar facility in Hong Kong. Committed to BC Hydro: no data/crypto mining. Potential 70,000L/day water usage for cooling. No subsea cables to Nanaimo currently -- nearest is Google Topaz cable landing at Port Alberni (40km NW). Not AI-focused.
notes: Operator identity not publicly confirmed. Very early stage proposal. Water-intensive cooling suggests conventional air/chilled water design. Subsea connectivity gap is significant barrier. BC Hydro clean energy. Track for operator identification and cooling design decisions.

opportunity_summary: First Canadian colocation DC to implement large-scale fresh-air cooling. 231 Amber St, Markham ON. Completed 2015. Carrier neutral. Modular AC, transformers, UPS. Variable-speed fans and smart coils reduce fan energy 20-35%. Advocates for DLC and rear-door heat exchangers for high-density. Potential liquid cooling upgrade candidate.
notes: Address: 231 Amber St, Markham ON L3R 3J7. Pioneer of free air cooling in Canadian colo market. Blog discusses liquid cooling benefits (25-40% efficiency gains in dense clusters) suggesting future interest. No MW or PUE figures publicly disclosed. Small operator but technically forward-thinking on cooling.

opportunity_summary: 16MW facility at 45 Parliament Street, Toronto. 3,000+ tons mechanical cooling from magnetic bearing oil-free chillers. Programmable logic controllers. Maximized free cooling cycle. Equinix expanding DLC (direct-to-chip) to 100+ DCs globally including Toronto, Calgary, Kamloops. Liquid-to-cage product offering available. 45+ metros already commercialized.
notes: DEDUP: Overlaps with existing 'Equinix TR2 Toronto' -- this entry ADDS liquid cooling expansion details: 16MW capacity, 3000+ tons mechanical cooling, magnetic bearing oil-free chillers, DLC rollout to Toronto/Calgary/Kamloops, 45+ metros commercialized globally, ZutaCore partnership, liquid-to-cage product. Merge into existing record. Cold plates on chips with liquid supply/return channels. Standard IT cabinet compatible.
